## Call Script

[OPENER — 1-2 lines]

[ASK]

[SOFT CLOSE — price anchor + deposit]
The full site — yours to own outright, hosted, with your real logo, photos, and any tweaks you want — runs $1,500. We do half, $750, as a deposit to get started, and the rest when we go live on your own domain. Most clients are live within 48 hours.
[Soft close question]
Does it make sense to get you set up this week, before the summer AC rush hits?

## Value bullets (for any talking-point moment)

- You've earned a strong reputation in Henry County over 12+ years — but right now a homeowner who hears your name has nowhere to land when they Google you. The site gives that reputation a home and keeps the customer from drifting to a competitor with a website.
- One tap → the phone rings at (770) 349-9809. On a 95-degree day, the HVAC company a homeowner can call in one tap is the one that wins the job. The site puts that button on every screen.
- The contact form captures the lead while you're up on a roof or under a house. Every after-hours request that would've gone to voicemail (or a competitor) becomes a message waiting for you.
- Free-estimate offer is front and center, which is exactly what a homeowner weighing a system replacement is searching for.

---

## Objection handling

**I already get plenty of work from word-of-mouth and referrals.**
→ Totally — referrals are how you built a 12-year reputation. The site catches the people who got your name from a neighbor but then Googled you before calling. No website often reads as "are they still in business?" — the page closes that gap so the referral actually turns into a call.

**I don't have time to deal with a website.**
→ That's the whole point — we already built it. You don't manage it, you don't update it. Maybe half an hour of your time total: look over the copy, send me your logo and a couple of job photos, and we publish.

**$1,500 is too much / money's tight.**
→ Fair. One captured service call or estimate covers most of it — and a single system replacement covers it many times over. If the site books even one job this season you'd otherwise have missed, it's already paid for itself.

**I don't really do anything online / I'm not techy.**
→ You don't have to be. There's nothing for you to log into. It's a phone-first page — the big button just calls your existing number. We handle the technical side completely.
